title: Implement indexer backfill mechanism
markdown:
## Goal

Implement a backfill mechanism that paginates through an existing B3nd node's data and replays it through the indexer. This is needed when creating a new indexer on existing data, or when rebuilding an indexer after schema changes.

## Background

When a new indexer is deployed against a node that already has thousands of records, those records need to be indexed. The backfill mechanism:

1. Lists all URIs matching the indexer's URI patterns
2. Reads each record
3. Passes it through the indexer's extract function
4. Writes to the indexer backend

This must be paginated (the node's `list()` supports `page`/`limit`) and resumable (track progress via cursor store).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `backfillIndexer(options)` async function:
  - `source: NodeProtocolInterface` -- the node to read from
  - `backend: IndexerBackend` -- where to write indexed data
  - `definition: IndexerDefinition` -- the indexer definition
  - `cursorStore?: CursorStore` -- for resumable backfill
  - `batchSize?: number` (default 50)
  - `onProgress?: (processed: number, total: number) => void`
- [ ] Paginates through `source.list()` for each URI pattern in the definition
- [ ] Reads each record and passes through `definition.extract()`
- [ ] Writes extracted fields to backend via `backend.index()`
- [ ] Saves progress to cursor store after each batch (resumable on restart)
- [ ] Returns `{ processed: number, skipped: number, errors: number, duration: number }`
- [ ] Handles extraction errors gracefully (logs, increments error count, continues)
- [ ] Tests: backfill from MemoryClient, resumable backfill, error handling

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-core/types.ts` for `ListResult`, `ListOptions` pagination
2. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/backfill.ts`:
   - For each URI pattern, call `source.list(pattern, { page, limit: batchSize })`
   - For each item in the list, call `source.read(item.uri)`
   - Run `definition.extract(uri, record.data)`
   - If extraction returns non-null, call `backend.index(uri, fields)`
   - After each batch, save cursor to `cursorStore.set(definition.name, lastUri)`
   - On resume, skip URIs already processed (compare against stored cursor)
3. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/backfill_test.ts`:
   - Seed a MemoryClient with test records
   - Run backfill
   - Query the backend to verify all records were indexed
   - Test resumability: interrupt halfway, resume, verify completion
4. Export from `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts`

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-indexer/backfill.ts` -- new: backfill implementation
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/backfill_test.ts` -- new: tests
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts` -- add export

## Testing

```bash
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-indexer/backfill_test.ts
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A6-03 (processor/extract pattern), G1-A6-04 (cursor store)
- **Blocks**: G1-A6-11 (schema evolution uses backfill for rebuild)
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Implement `IndexerDefinition` interface and runtime
- [ ] Implement `IndexerBackend` — at least one backend (SQLite or Postgres)
- [ ] Implement `createIndexerProcessor` — compose `Processor` that pipes messages to indexer
- [ ] Implement message feed / event stream (pull-based cursor, `GET /api/v1/feed?since={cursor}`)
- [ ] Implement backfill mechanism (paginate through source node, replay through indexer)
- [ ] Implement cursor store (using B3nd node itself as storage)
- [ ] Implement `
